# vessel-traffic-mcp

> Read-only Model Context Protocol server for vessel identity,
> AIS-style position data, carrier schedules, vessel schedules, and
> delay heuristics.

Repository: https://github.com/tools-mcp/vessel-traffic-mcp
Agent discovery page: https://tools-mcp.github.io/vessel-traffic-mcp/
License: MIT
Runtime: Node.js 22+, TypeScript, ESM
Default transport: stdio for local MCP clients
Remote transport: Streamable HTTP at `/mcp`, with public `/health`

## Agent Setup Task

When an agent is asked to install this MCP server on a new machine, do
the following:

1. Clone `https://github.com/tools-mcp/vessel-traffic-mcp.git`.
2. Run `npm ci`.
3. Run `npm run build`.
4. Register the built server as a local stdio MCP server with an
   absolute path to `dist/index.js`.
5. Set `VESSEL_MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio`.
6. For public opt-in providers, set
   `VESSEL_MCP_ENABLE_PUBLIC_PROVIDERS=myshiptracking,tradlinx`.
   Add `shipfinder` only when the user accepts browser-verification
   no-data responses from that provider.
7. Restart the MCP client.
8. Verify by asking for `EVER GIVEN` current position with source URL,
   and for a carrier schedule from `KRPUS` to `NLRTM` with source URL.

## Search-Style Agent Flow

For agents or products that expect generic connector tools, use:

1. `search` with query `EVER GIVEN`.
2. `fetch` with the returned `vessel:mmsi:*`, `vessel:imo:*`, or
   `vessel:name:*` id.
3. Include `source.provider`, `source.landingUrl`, `observedAt`,
   `retrievedAt`, `freshnessSeconds`, confidence, and coverage caveats.
4. State that AIS-style data is not for safety-critical navigation.

## Codex CLI Config

Add this to the local `~/.codex/config.toml`, replacing only the
absolute path:

```toml
[mcp_servers.vessel-traffic-mcp]
command = "node"
args = ["/absolute/path/to/vessel-traffic-mcp/dist/index.js"]

[mcp_servers.vessel-traffic-mcp.env]
VESSEL_MCP_TRANSPORT = "stdio"
VESSEL_MCP_ENABLE_PUBLIC_PROVIDERS = "myshiptracking,tradlinx"
```

## Claude Desktop / Claude Code Config

Add this MCP server entry to the local Claude config, replacing only
the absolute path:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "vessel-traffic-mcp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/vessel-traffic-mcp/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "VESSEL_MCP_TRANSPORT": "stdio",
        "VESSEL_MCP_ENABLE_PUBLIC_PROVIDERS": "myshiptracking,tradlinx"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Safety Rules

- Keep all MCP tools read-only.
- Do not bypass authentication, paywalls, CAPTCHA, bot defenses, rate
  limits, or access controls.
- Do not commit local MCP client configs, `.env` files, cookies,
  bearer tokens, API keys, HAR files, browser sessions, or raw
  captures.
- Do not copy credentials from another machine.
- Paid providers are BYOK only. Configure API keys only through local
  environment variables or ignored local config files.
- Every vessel or schedule response from a live/public provider must
  expose provenance: `source.provider` and `source.landingUrl`.
- This is not a safety-critical navigation tool.

## Provider Status

- Default runtime: `fixture`.
- Public opt-in runtime providers:
  `myshiptracking`, `shipfinder`, `tradlinx-schedule`.
- Implemented credentialed runtime providers:
  `marinetraffic`, `vesselfinder`, `aisstream`, `aishub`,
  `barentswatch`, `searates-schedules`, `routescanner-connect`,
  `vesselapi`, `datadocked`, `datalastic`, `globalfishingwatch`.
- Planned schedule providers:
  `linescape-schedule-api`.
- The remaining PRD providers are cataloged for discovery, enterprise
  review, or future BYOK adapter work. Do not claim they are fully
  implemented until `config/provider-catalog.example.json` marks them
  `implemented` and runtime registry can enable them.

## Main Tools

- `vessel_search`
- `vessel_position`
- `vessel_area`
- `vessel_track`
- `port_calls`
- `carrier_schedule_search`
- `vessel_schedule`
- `schedule_delay_predict`
- `provider_status`
- `data_sources`
- `credential_profiles`
- `provider_onboarding`
- `search`
- `fetch`

## Important Docs

- `README.md`: full setup, provider notes, and agent install prompt.
- `docs/index.html`: static page for ChatGPT, Codex, Claude, Gemini,
  and search-engine discovery.
- `server.json`: MCP Registry metadata for
  `io.github.tools-mcp/vessel-traffic-mcp`.
- `AGENTS.md`: non-negotiable repository rules.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: collaboration expectations.
- `docs/runbooks/public-sharing.md`: GitHub, MCP Registry, Smithery,
  Glama, PulseMCP, and launch-post sharing checklist.
- `docs/runbooks/clients.md`: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, ChatGPT
  remote MCP, and MCP Inspector setup.
- `docs/runbooks/codex.md`: Codex CLI setup.
- `docs/runbooks/operator.md`: operational runbook.
- `docs/provider-catalog.md`: provider inventory and routing policy.
- `docs/runbooks/credential-profiles.md`: BYOK credential profile
  handling.
- `docs/runbooks/browser-api-capture-results.md`: sanitized public
  vessel API capture notes.
- `docs/runbooks/schedule-api-capture-results.md`: sanitized schedule
  API capture notes.
